title: xript Manifest
description: Declarative manifest for xript-enabled applications. Defines the API surface, capabilities, and documentation for modders.

$schema.description: Optional JSON Schema reference for editor autocomplete. Should point to the xript manifest schema URL.

xript.description: The xript specification version this manifest conforms to.

name.description: Machine-readable identifier for this application. Used in generated package names and documentation URLs.

version.description: The version of this application's scripting API. Follows semver.

title.description: Human-readable display name for the application.

description.description: A brief description of what this application does and what modders can extend.

bindings.description: The functions and namespaces exposed to scripts. This is the API surface.

capabilities.description: Named capabilities that scripts can request. Each capability gates access to a set of bindings or behaviors.

types.description: Custom type definitions referenced by bindings. Enables rich type generation and documentation.

hooks.description: Hooks that scripts can register handlers for. Hooks are fired by the host and handled by scripts — the reverse direction of bindings.

typeRef.description: A reference to a type. Can be a primitive, a custom type name, an array type, or a union.

oneOf.description: A primitive type name, custom type reference, or shorthand.

map.description: A string-keyed map with values of this type.

optional.description: An optional (nullable) version of this type.

parameter.description: A function parameter with a name, type, and optional description.

default.description: The default value if the parameter is omitted. Implies the parameter is optional.

required.description: Whether this parameter is required. Defaults to true unless a default value is provided.

binding.description: A function or namespace exposed to scripts.

functionBinding.description: A callable function exposed to scripts.

description.description: What this function does. Shown in generated docs and editor tooltips.

returns.description: The return type. Omit for void functions.

async.description: Whether this function is asynchronous. Defaults to false.

capability.description: The capability required to call this function. If omitted, the function is always available.

deprecated.description: If present, this function is deprecated. The value describes the migration path.

namespaceBinding.description: A namespace that groups related functions.

members.description: The functions and sub-namespaces in this namespace.

capability.description: A named capability that gates access to functionality.

description.description: What this capability grants access to. Shown to the user when a script requests it.

risk.description: Advisory risk level. Helps users make informed decisions about granting capabilities.

fields.description: The fields of this type (for object/struct types).

fieldDefinition.description: A field within a custom type.

optional.description: Whether this field can be absent. Defaults to false.

title.description: A short title for this example.

description.description: Explanation of what this example demonstrates.

hook.description: A hook that scripts can register handlers for. Hooks are the reverse of bindings: the host fires them, and scripts handle them.

description.description: What this hook represents and when it fires. Shown in generated docs.

phases.description: Lifecycle phases for this hook. If omitted, the hook is a simple notification with no lifecycle.

params.description: The parameters passed to hook handlers when fired.

capability.description: The capability required to register for this hook. If omitted, any script can register.

async.description: Whether handlers run asynchronously. Host-controlled. Defaults to false.

limits.description: Per-handler execution limits. Overrides the manifest-level defaults for this hook's handlers.

deprecated.description: If present, this hook is deprecated. The value describes the migration path.

executionLimits.description: Default execution limits for scripts. Runtimes enforce these limits to prevent denial of service.
